Jupiter's years commence from Karttika and consist of two
asterisms each beginning with Krttika and follow in regular

82 Brhat Samhitu
order. However, the 5th, 10th and 11th years comprise each
three asterisms.
[Here the word Antya is interpreted as the 12th to the
11th i.e. 10th. If Jupiter rises in krttika or Rohinl, his year will be
called Knriiika; if in Mrga^iras_or Ardra, Margaslrsz-, if in Punar-
vasu or Pu§ya, Pausa; if in Aslesa or Maghs, Mugha, if in
Purvaphalgum, Uttaraphalgum or Hasta, Phalguna; if in CitrS or
SvStl, Caitra; if in Visakha or Anuradha, VaisUkha; if_in Jye§tha
or Mula, jyetfha; if in Purva§adha or Uttarasadha, AsHdha; if in
Sravana, Dhanistha, or Satabhisaj, Sravana; if in Purvabhadra,
